name: Traditional Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour, salt, and baking soda together into a medium mixing bowl.
2. Mix in the shortening using your fingertips. Gradually add buttermilk until combined.
3. Form dough into a soft ball. Turn out onto a well-floured surface.
4. Knead just until smooth and firm enough to handle (no more than 5 minutes).
5. Roll or pat dough to a thickness of 1/2 inch. Cut with floured biscuit cutters, knife, or the rim of a smooth drinking glass.
6. Place biscuits on a greased baking sheet.
7. Bake in a pre-heated oven at 400°F for 10 minutes, until golden brown.
Hint: For biscuit cutters, use empty cans with holes punched in the bottoms to release air. #303 cans are ideal for large biscuits, and tomato sauce cans (8 oz.) work well for small ones.
